versions.version_description: This version contain the colored chords needed to come close to the original song and melody performed by Lisa Stansfield. Please pay attention to that the guitar or key board needs to be tuned down around 5 cent or approx one quarter of a halv tone.

tuning: E A D G B E

pro_brother.version_description: Guitar Pro tab for vocal, synth, bass, piano, strings and drums (ey-ya-yye).

content:
Notes:
The recorded version of this is in a weird hinterland tuning which is a tiny bit lower than this.
I like to play the G6 as a "slid-up" variation of the Fmaj7, e.g:
[ch]Fmaj7[/ch] = 133210
[tab][ch]G6[/ch] = 355430
... but an "easy" G6 still sounds OK.[/tab]
And then just lean into the G on the top string to make it briefly major during the verse.
If you like, you can briefly hammer off-then-on the B string during Fmaj7 and Am7 chords for a bit of sass.
The E9sus4 sounds best as x77777


[Spoken Intro]
[tab][ch]C#maj7[/ch]
I don't know where my baby is[/tab]
[tab][ch]D#6[/ch]
But I'll find him[/tab]
[tab][ch]C#maj7[/ch]
Somewhere, somehow[/tab]
[tab][ch]D#6[/ch] [ch]C#maj7[/ch]
    I've got to let him know how much I care[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]D#6[/ch]                          [ch]Bm7[/ch]
I'll never give up looking for my baby[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]Fmaj7[/ch]
Been around the world and I, I, I[/tab]
[tab]        [ch]G6[/ch]
I can't find my baby[/tab]
[tab][ch]Fmaj7[/ch]

[tab]         [ch]G6[/ch]
